title: Integrate Centralized Validation Middleware for Image and Print Routes
body:
#192 
I extend the existing backend validation architecture to image processing and print generation routes using express-validator and the shared validate.middleware.js.  in tis PR.
While reviewing the backend structure, I noticed that authentication routes were already using centralized validation middleware, but image and print routes were still handling request validation manually inside controllers.

This refactor standardizes validation handling across backend APIs and improves separation of concerns.

### **Changes Made**
Added Dedicated Validation Files
Created:

> backend/src/validation/image.validation.js
> backend/src/validation/print.validation.js

These files now contain centralized request validation rules for:

- required filename validation
- allowed background colour validation
- supported photo size preset validation
- quantity range validation for print generation
- Route Integration

**Integrated validation middleware into:**

> image.routes.js
> print.routes.js

Updated routes now follow the existing backend validation architecture pattern:

router.post("/", processImageValidation, validate, processImage);

**Controller Cleanup**

Removed manual request validation checks from:

> image.controller.js
> print.controller.js

Controllers now focus only on business logic while request validation is handled before controller execution.

title: feat(admin): complete admin backend & interactive dashboard integration
body:
This PR introduces the complete end-to-end flow for the Admin Dashboard, bridging the React frontend with a secure Express backend. It transitions the admin dashboard from a static UI into a fully dynamic and interactive management console.

### Technical Implementation
## Backend:

Admin Controllers & Routes: Added endpoints to fetch aggregate statistics (/api/admin/stats), retrieve paginated upload history (/api/admin/uploads), and manage application settings (/api/admin/settings).
Database Models:
Created a new Settings Mongoose model to store dynamic application configuration (Max Upload Size, API URLs, Maintenance Mode) persistently in MongoDB, removing the need for server restarts when updating configuration.
Added a pre('save') hook on the User model to automatically handle secure bcrypt hashing.
Security & Seed Script: Configured robust JWT handling with httpOnly cookies and fixed the createAdmin.js seed script to correctly auto-hash the default admin password.

## Frontend:

Recharts Integration: Installed and configured recharts to render a dynamic line chart on the Overview tab, plotting the volume of total vs. completed uploads over the trailing 7-day period.
Interactive Settings UI: Converted the Settings tab into a fully functional React form mapped directly to the PUT /api/admin/settings endpoint.
Component Refactoring: Cleaned up AdminDashboard.jsx, breaking the UI down into modular sub-components (OverviewTab, UploadsTab, SettingsTab) and cleaning up duplicated legacy code.
Resilience: Added .trim() validation on the AdminLogin.jsx to prevent invisible whitespace from blocking login attempts.


## How to Test
Initialize Admin Credentials: Run npm run dev in the backend, or explicitly run the seed script:
```bash
node backend/src/seed/createAdmin.js
```


- Access the Dashboard: Start the frontend (npm run dev), navigate to /admin/login, and sign in using admin **(Initialize an admin user via seed script or existing database setup)**
- Verify Settings: Navigate to the "Settings" tab, update the Max Upload Size or enable Maintenance Mode, click "Save Changes", and refresh to verify data persistence in MongoDB.
- Verify Analytics: Generate a few test uploads and check the "Overview" tab to see the line chart instantly map the historical data.

title: fix: Define global CSS design tokens in index.css
body:
🛠️ GSSoC 2026 Pull Request: Global CSS Design Tokens Definition

📝 Description / Rationale

This PR resolves a critical frontend styling issue where multiple components reference CSS custom properties (design tokens) such as:

  • --color-primary

  • --color-border

  • --radius-lg

  • --color-bg

but these variables were never globally defined anywhere in the frontend codebase.

Because undefined CSS variables fall back to invalid/initial values, several UI elements could render incorrectly across browsers, leading to:

  • Invisible borders

  • Transparent backgrounds

  • Missing spacing/radius styles

  • Inconsistent component rendering

  • Broken UI layouts

Additionally, the project's contribution guidelines explicitly require contributors to use design tokens instead of hardcoded values, but no centralized source of truth existed.

This PR introduces a complete global token system inside frontend/src/index.css to standardize styling and improve long-term maintainability.

Closes #196

💡 Proposed Technical Changes

Target File

  • frontend/src/index.css

Logic Implemented

✅ Added centralized :root design token definitions

Introduced global CSS variables for:

  • Brand colors

  • Background colors

  • Text colors

  • Borders

  • Border radius

  • Spacing scale

  • Shadows

  • Typography

  • Transition timings

✅ Added dark mode token overrides

Implemented foundational dark mode support using:

@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)

This enables future dark-theme compatibility without requiring component-level refactors.

✅ Standardized token architecture

Established a single source of truth for frontend styling across all components.

🛡️ Safeguards Added

  • Prevents undefined CSS variable rendering issues

  • Ensures consistent UI styling across browsers/devices

  • Improves maintainability for future contributors

  • Reduces hardcoded style duplication

  • Enables scalable future theming support

  • Maintains compatibility with existing component styles

🧪 Local Quality Assurance & Verification

  • Verified borders/backgrounds render consistently

  • Confirmed no regressions in UploadBox and shared UI components

  • Tested dark mode token overrides

  • Verified zero breaking mutations to adjacent components

📊 Result After Fix

Scenario | Before | After -- | -- | -- Undefined CSS variables | Components rendered inconsistently | All tokens resolved globally Borders/backgrounds | Missing or transparent | Properly rendered Contribution guideline compliance | Incomplete | Fully supported Theme scalability | Difficult | Centralized token system Dark mode readiness | Not supported | Base support added

title: fix(middleware): protect production env from stack trace leaks
body:
### Description
This pull request prevents potential sensitive internal server leakage by standardizing how the environment (`NODE_ENV`) is evaluated within the error-handling middleware.

### The Problem
In `backend/src/middleware/error.middleware.js`, the server's error stack trace is returned to the client based on direct access to `process.env.NODE_ENV`:
```javascript
stack: process.env.NODE_ENV === "development" ? err.stack : undefined,
```
This direct bypass of the centralized config layer:
1. Is highly fragile, as raw environment variables might not be normalized (e.g., if `NODE_ENV` is unset, `process.env.NODE_ENV` is `undefined`, causing inconsistent behaviors and schema validation errors compared to the rest of the application).
2. Risks disclosing internal server file structures, SQL/NoSQL query errors, and environment details if the environment variable setup undergoes minor configuration mismatches.

Standardizing on `config.NODE_ENV` guarantees that the application consistently knows whether it is in development mode or not.

### The Fix
- Imported the centralized config from `../config/config.js`.
- Replaced the direct evaluation of `process.env.NODE_ENV` with `config.NODE_ENV`.

---
**Closes #206**

*I am contributing on behalf of GSSoc'26*

title: fix(otp): remove typo from password reset OTP state enum
body:
### Description
This pull request resolves a data validation loophole in the password reset OTP schema where an invalid `"rejecte"` state value was accidentally allowed due to a copy-paste/typing bug.

### The Problem
In `backend/src/models/passwordResetOtp.model.js`, the enum validation for the `state` field contains a typo `"rejecte"`:
```javascript
state: {
  type: String,
  enum: ["pending", "resolve", "reject", "rejecte"],
  default: "pending",
}
```
This bypasses strict data constraints and allows invalid states to persist in the database, compromising schema predictability and data integrity during reporting, verification, and audit cleanups.

### The Fix
- Removed `"rejecte"` from the mongoose enum array so that only `"pending"`, `"resolve"`, and `"reject"` are valid states.

---
**Closes #204**

*I am contributing on behalf of GSSoc'26*

title: fix(auth): resolve password hashing inconsistency and lockout risk
body:
### Description
This pull request resolves a critical defect in the password reset / update flow where the user password could be double-hashed and result in permanent user account lockout, or bypass Mongoose `save` hooks.

### The Problem
1. The `updatePassword` function in `auth.service.js` manually hashes the new password with `bcrypt.hash()` before calling the DAO layer.
2. The DAO layer (`updateUserPassword` in `user.dao.js`) uses `User.findByIdAndUpdate(...)` to save the password. This database operation bypasses the Mongoose `pre('save')` middleware entirely, including any standard lifecycle validation/sanitization.
3. If the codebase is later updated, or if `user.save()` is called in the lifecycle, the `pre('save')` hook on `user.model.js` (which performs hashing) will execute, resulting in double-hashing the password (i.e. hashing the already-hashed password). This renders the password unusable and permanently locks the user out of their account.

### The Fix
1. Removed manual `bcrypt.hash()` execution in the `auth.service.js` layer.
2. Rewrote `updateUserPassword` in `user.dao.js` to utilize the modern `findById` + `save()` pattern. This ensures Mongoose `pre('save')` hooks run consistently for all password updates.
3. This guarantees validation is consistently applied and prevents any future double-hashing scenario.

---
**Closes #202**

*I am contributing on behalf of GSSoc'26*

title: fix(security): hash password reset OTP with bcrypt before database storage
body:
### Summary

This PR fixes a **critical credential storage vulnerability** where the 6-digit password reset OTP was stored as **plaintext** in MongoDB.

If the database is ever compromised (injection, backup leak, insider threat, misconfigured access control), an attacker could read every pending OTP and reset any user's password — achieving **mass account takeover**.

### Changes

In `backend/src/service/passwordResetOtp.service.js`:

1. **Hash before storage**: OTP is now hashed with `bcrypt` (cost factor 10) before being persisted to the database
2. **Secure comparison**: Replaced direct string comparison (`!==`) with `bcrypt.compare()` during verification
3. **Plaintext only for email**: The unhashed OTP is returned only on the in-memory object for the email dispatch and is never persisted

This aligns with OWASP guidelines that mandate OTPs be treated identically to passwords for storage purposes.

Closes #200

---
I am contributing on behalf of GSSoC'26.

title: fix(security): prevent path traversal in image processing endpoint
body:
### Summary

This PR fixes a **critical path traversal vulnerability (CWE-22)** in the `/api/process` endpoint.

The `filename` parameter from the request body was directly concatenated into the file path without sanitization, allowing an attacker to read **any file on the server** by sending traversal sequences like `../../.env`.

### Changes

- Added `path.basename()` sanitization to strip directory components from user-supplied `filename` before constructing the file path in `image.controller.js`.

### Before (vulnerable)
```javascript
const filePath = path.join(__dirname, "..", "..", "uploads", filename);
```

### After (safe)
```javascript
const safeName = path.basename(filename);
const filePath = path.join(__dirname, "..", "..", "uploads", safeName);
```

Closes #198

---
I am contributing on behalf of GSSoC'26.

title: fix(ai-service): save and return fully processed final_image instead of result_bytes
body:
### Summary
This PR fixes a critical bug in `/remove-bg` route of the Flask AI service where the face detection/centering and DPI resize optimization steps were completely bypassed when returning the processed image.

### Details
In the handler, the image is correctly processed step-by-step:
1. `remove_background` (yields `result_bytes`)
2. `center_face` (yields `centered`)
3. `optimise_dpi` (yields `final_image`)

However, when writing the processed image to the local upload directory (`save_path`), the code saved `result_bytes` (un-centered and un-optimized image) instead of `final_image`. As a result, the client received the raw background-removed image instead of the face-centered, high-resolution printable passport photo.

This PR updates the write statement to save the `final_image` to the designated path.

### Reference
Closes #187

---
I am contributing on behalf of GSSoc'26.

title: fix(ai-service): resolve photo preset key mismatch with backend
body:
### Summary
This PR fixes a parameter key mismatch between the Node.js Express backend and the Flask AI service. The Express backend sends the photo preset identifier as `photo_size_preset` in a form-data request, but the Flask AI service was reading `preset = request.form.get("preset")`. Because of this key mismatch, the user's selected photo sizing preset was completely ignored and always defaulted to `"35x45"`.

### Changes
- Updated the Flask `process_routes.py` endpoint to read the preset from either `preset` or `photo_size_preset` with a fallback to `"35x45"`.
- This ensures full backwards compatibility with any other callers while resolving the integration bug with the Express backend.

### Reference
Closes #185

---
I am contributing on behalf of GSSoc'26.

title: implement dark mode toggle and UI contrast as per the Assigned task
body:
#  SnapPass-AI: Dark Mode Implementation Guide

To implement a fully responsive global Light/Dark mode toggle that respects user preferences, stores local state, and blends seamlessly across the entire user interface to enhance accessibility and user experience.

##  The Problem
Prior to this implementation:
- The application lacked a native Dark Mode option.
- Hardcoded values prevented the application from scaling its color palette dynamically, causing severe contrast conflicts when a darker aesthetic was applied.

## The Solution
A robust theme-switching architecture was introduced using **React Context** and **CSS Variables**.

### 1. Global State Management (`ThemeContext`)
- **File:** `src/context/ThemeContext.jsx`
- **Purpose:** Manages the global theme state (`light` or `dark`).
- **Features:**
  - **System Preferences:** Automatically detects the user's system preference via `window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')` to provide a smart default.
  - **Persistence:** Caches the user's manual selection in the browser's `localStorage` so the preference persists across page reloads.
  - **DOM Injection:** Dynamically injects a `data-theme` attribute into the `<html>` root element, allowing CSS to target global changes effortlessly.

### 2. CSS Variable Architecture
- **File:** `src/index.css`
- **Purpose:** Centralized design tokens and color palettes.
- **Features:** 
  - Restructured variables (`--color-bg`, `--color-surface`, `--color-text`, etc.).
  - Added a `[data-theme='dark']` block that overrides global variables with a carefully curated dark palette (utilizing slate, zinc, and blue hues) to maintain brand identity while ensuring high contrast.

### 3. UI Toggle Integration
- **Files:** `src/components/layout/Navbar.jsx` & `src/components/layout/Navbar.css`
- **Action:** 
  - Integrated an interactive `Sun` and `Moon` icon toggle button (via `lucide-react`) within the Navbar.
  - Removed hardcoded black/white text and background colors in `Navbar.css`, substituting them with semantic CSS variables.

---

## How to Add New Components in the Future
To ensure future components support Dark Mode out-of-the-box:
 **Always use Semantic Variables:**
   - Backgrounds: `var(--color-bg)` or `var(--color-surface)`
   - Text: `var(--color-text)` or `var(--color-text-muted)`
   - Borders: `var(--color-border)`
   - Brand Colors: `var(--color-primary)`

By adhering to these variables, any new component will instantly inherit both Light and Dark mode styling perfectly!

title: fix: reset RouteErrorBoundary on route change using location key
body:
Fixes #128 

## What was the problem?
When a lazy-loaded page failed to load, the error boundary would catch 
it and show the fallback UI. But since hasError never reset on navigation, 
clicking any navbar link did nothing — the user was stuck.

## What's the fix?
Added key={location.pathname} to RouteErrorBoundary in AppRoutes.jsx. 
This forces the boundary to remount on every route change, clearing 
the error state automatically.

## Changes
- AppRoutes.jsx — added useLocation import + key prop on the boundary

## Demo
before fixing :https://drive.google.com/file/d/1WCDqHaKCTr8CuEUJCEUrnG0LPmlpmAoy/view?usp=drive_link
after fixing the bug : https://drive.google.com/file/d/1NULGzqizTsUKUJASFX88xMV8RRoNvUqZ/view?usp=sharing

## Labels
level:beginner · type:bug

title: Implement Password Reset Flow with OTP Verification via Email
body:
# Implement Password Reset Flow with OTP Verification via Email

## Overview

This PR adds a complete password reset workflow using OTP verification via email.

The implementation introduces secure password reset functionality with OTP generation, verification, and password update support. Email delivery is handled using Resend.

---

## Changes Made

### Added OTP-Based Password Reset Flow

Implemented the following features:

- Request password reset via email
- Generate and send OTP to user email
- Verify OTP before allowing password reset
- Reset password securely after OTP verification

---

## New Routes

```js
/**
 * @route POST /api/auth/password-reset-request
 * @description Request an OTP for password reset
 * @access Public
 */
router.post(
  "/password-reset-request",
  passwordResetRequestValidation,
  validate,
  authController.requestPasswordReset
);

/**
 * @route POST /api/auth/verify-otp
 * @description Verify the OTP before resetting the password
 * @access Public
 */
router.post(
  "/verify-otp",
  verifyPasswordResetOtpValidation,
  validate,
  authController.verifyPasswordResetOtp
);

/**
```

title: Implement Authentication System with JWT and Protected Routes
body:
# PR: Implement Authentication System with JWT and Protected Routes

## Description

This PR introduces a complete authentication system for the backend using JWT-based authentication.

The implementation includes:

- User registration
- User login
- Password hashing using bcrypt
- JWT token generation
- Authentication middleware
- Protected routes
- Request validation
- Centralized authentication error handling

The goal of this PR is to establish a secure and scalable authentication architecture for the application.

---

# Features Added

## User Registration

Implemented user signup functionality with:

- Email validation
- Password validation
- Duplicate user checking
- Password hashing using `bcrypt`
- User creation in database

### Registration Flow

1. Validate request body
2. Check if user already exists
3. Hash password
4. Save user to database
5. Return success response

---

## User Login

Implemented login functionality with:

- Email/password verification
- Secure password comparison
- JWT token generation
- Standardized response handling

### Login Flow

1. Validate request body
2. Find user by email
3. Compare password using bcrypt
4. Generate JWT token
5. Return authentication response

---

## JWT Authentication

Added JWT-based authentication system.

### Includes

- Token generation utility
- Token verification middleware
- Protected route support
- User authentication handling

---

## Authentication Middleware

Created middleware to:

- Extract JWT token
- Verify token validity
- Attach authenticated user to request
- Block unauthorized access

### Example

```js
export const authMiddleware= async (req, res, next) => {
  // verify token
};
```

---

## Password Security

Passwords are securely hashed using bcrypt before storing in the database.

### Security Improvements

- Prevents plain text password storage
- Uses secure password comparison
- Improves user data protection

---

## Validation Added

Added request validation using `express-validator`.

### Validated Fields

- Email format
- Password strength
- Required fields
- Authentication request structure

---

# Project Structure

```bash
src/
├── config/
├── controllers/
│   └── auth.controller.js
│
├── services/
│   └── auth.service.js
│
├── dao/
│   └── user.dao.js
│
├── middleware/
│   └── auth.middleware.js
│
├── routes/
│   └── auth.routes.js
│
├── validation/
│   └── auth.validation.js
│
├── utils/
│   ├── setToken.js
│   └── errors/
│
└── models/
    └── user.model.js
```

---

# API Endpoints Added

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| POST | `/api/auth/register` | Register new user |
| POST | `/api/auth/login` | Login existing user |
| POST | `/api/auth/logout` | logout current user |
| GET | `/api/auth/me` | Get authenticated user profile |

---

# Example Responses

## Registration Success

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "User registered successfully",
  "data": {
    "user": {},
    "token": ""
  }
}
```

---

## Login Success

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Login successful",
  "data": {
    "user": {},
    "token": ""
  }
}
```

---

## Unauthorized Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "message": "Unauthorized access"
}
```

---

# Benefits

- Secure authentication architecture
- JWT-based stateless authentication
- Reusable authentication middleware
- Scalable backend authentication flow
- Clean separation of concerns
- Improved API security

---

# Future Improvements

- Refresh token support
- Role-based authorization
- Email verification
- Forgot/reset password flow
- OAuth authentication
- Rate limiting for auth routes

---

# Checklist

- [x] Added user registration
- [x] Added user login
- [x] Added JWT authentication
- [x] Added password hashing
- [x] Added protected routes
- [x] Added request validation
- [x] Added authentication middleware
- [x] Added standardized API responses
- [x] Followed backend architecture conventions

Closes #11

title: Backend Model Schemas for Cloud Storage Integration
body:
PR: Backend Model Schemas for Cloud Storage Integration

Description

This PR introduces new Mongoose model schemas for the SnapPass AI backend, designed to support a cloud-storage-agnostic architecture. The models replace the previous basic schemas and establish the data foundation for the core photo upload → processing → print sheet workflow.

Changes

New Models Created

1. user.model.js - User authentication & profile management
   - fullName, email (unique), passwordHash
   - role (user/admin), isActive, lastLoginAt
   - Timestamps, unique email index

2. upload.model.js - Uploaded photo metadata
   - Reference to user
   - fileId (unique), originalName, fileUrl (cloud link only)
   - mimeType, sizeBytes, status (uploaded/processing/processed/failed)
   - lastError for failure tracking
   - Composite index on user + createdAt

3. processedImage.model.js - Processed photo records
   - References to user and upload
   - backgroundColour (white/light_blue/grey/custom)
   - photoSizePreset (35x45/51x51/33x48/40x60/2x2in)
   - outputUrl (cloud link only), widthPx, heightPx, dpi
   - status (queued/processing/completed/failed), processingTimeMs
   - errorMessage for failures

4. printSheet.model.js - A4 print sheet generation records
   - References to user and processedImage
   - photoSizePreset, quantity (1-50), sheetFormat (A4)
   - outputUrl (cloud link only)
   - status (queued/generating/generated/failed), errorMessage

Files Removed

- Deleted old non-convention models: Upload.js, User.js, UploadHistory.js
- Updated import in upload.controller.js to use upload.model.js

Data Flow

User 
  |
  v
Upload (raw photo stored in cloud)
  |
  v
ProcessedImage (background removed, face centered, resized)
  |
  v
PrintSheet (A4 layout with multiple copies)

Each stage maintains independent status tracking and error logging.

Cloud Storage Design

Database stores ONLY public URLs - storage provider details (S3, GCS, Cloudinary, etc.) are handled by the backend service layer, not persisted in DB. This keeps the models provider-agnostic and future-proof.

Naming Convention

All models follow the modelname.model.js pattern as per backend-rules.md.

Testing

- Models are syntactically valid ES modules
- Mongoose schema definitions checked for consistency
- Ready for integration with service/DAO layer

Notes

- user.model.js includes passwordHash field (bcrypt hashing to be implemented in auth service)
- All status fields are indexed for efficient querying
- Composite indexes optimize user-specific history queries
- Schemas include timestamps: true for audit trails

Related Issues

Closes #[Database Schema Planning & Standardization #10]
Closes #10

This PR adds background removal functionality using rembg library with the following highlights: 1.Model: Integrates rembg for high accuracy background segmentation 2.Compositing: Uses PIL alpha composite to allow seamless background swapping without losing edge details 3.Flexibility: Supports both pre-defined colour constants (white, off-white, blue, grey) and custom Hex-code inputs like #ffffff 4.Flask endpoint /remove-bg added for testing the service ## Which Stage does this belong to?
